expectations.evidence: Creates a CardGrid React component with TypeScript types (CardGridItem, columns/gap props). The component renders a responsive grid of shadcn/ui Cards.
Uses shadcn/ui Card primitives (Card, CardHeader, CardTitle, CardDescription, CardContent, CardFooter) and Tailwind CSS grid utilities. Also references the cn utility from '@/lib/utils'.

Component is layout-focused with configurable props (columns, gap, items). Content slots (content, footer) accept ReactNode for composability. No business logic is present -- the component only handles rendering card layout with responsive grid configuration.

suggestions: Without the skill, the agent still produced a solid component using shadcn/ui and Tailwind, but did not reference project-specific design system docs (theme-tokens, naming conventions, folder structure).
The component includes hover effects (shadow-lg, image zoom) which are presentational but slightly beyond minimal layout.
eval_feedback.overall: The without_skill run for eval-20 produced a well-structured card grid that meets all three assertions. The prompt's explicit mention of shadcn/ui likely guided the agent to use the right tooling even without the skill. However, it lacks the structured workflow and design system awareness that the with_skill run demonstrated.
